assumptions: The question concerns hedge accounting in consolidated financial statements under IFRS 9 for foreign exchange risk arising from an intragroup dividend receivable.
The dividend receivable is a recognised item in the consolidated hedge-accounting analysis.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies fair value hedge accounting as a distinct hedge accounting model for recognised assets or liabilities exposed to changes in fair value attributable to a particular risk.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies cash flow hedge accounting as a distinct hedge accounting model for exposure to variability in cash flows, including highly probable forecast transactions.

approaches.label: Net investment hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 and IFRIC 16 identify hedge accounting for a net investment in a foreign operation as a separate hedge accounting model for foreign currency exposure.
